items.prompt: In a word-search branch where a cell cannot be reused, when should the current cell be marked visited?

options.text: After bounds and value checks pass, before exploring neighbors.
Before confirming the coordinates are inside the grid.
Only after all neighbor recursion finishes.
Visited state is unnecessary in path search.

feedback.reason: The current cell becomes part of the active path only after it is proven legal.
feedback.details: The mark must be visible to every descendant so the path cannot revisit the cell.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.before_bounds: Out-of-bounds memory cannot be marked safely.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.after_all_neighbors: Descendants could reuse the current cell.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.never: Cycles and cell reuse can violate the path contract.

items.prompt: Why is one global visited set usually incorrect when every starting cell should be allowed to begin an independent path search?

options.text: A cell used by one failed path may still be required by another path or starting position.
Global visited works only on large grids.
A set cannot store coordinates.
Recursive search always requires global visited state.

feedback.reason: Visited state belongs to the current path when cells may be reused across different candidate paths.
feedback.details: Marking and unmarking or branch-local copies restore availability between searches.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.grid_too_small: Grid size is not the issue.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.visited_cannot_store_cells: A set can store coordinates; the scope is wrong.

items.prompt: A grid word-search call has already matched every target character. What should happen before attempting another cell access?

options.text: Return success immediately.
Read another cell to confirm the path.
Erase the entire board.
Restart the outer search loop.

feedback.reason: The completion contract is based on target progress, not on making an extra move.
feedback.details: Checking completion first also avoids unnecessary out-of-bounds or sentinel accesses after the final character.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.read_next_cell: The target contract is already complete.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.clear_board: Completion does not require destructive cleanup.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.restart_start_cells: Success may be propagated under a find-one contract.

items.prompt: A cell is marked visited, all neighbor branches fail, and the function returns without unmarking it. What defect results?

options.text: Sibling paths can be incorrectly blocked from using that cell.
Only the order of duplicate outputs changes.
The board becomes sorted.
The target string becomes shorter.

feedback.reason: A failed branch must restore every path-local mark.
feedback.details: Otherwise, the parent no longer represents the same search state for its remaining neighbors.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.duplicate_output_only: The branch may lose valid paths entirely.

items.prompt: A problem permits four-directional movement only. Which neighbor set is legal from (r, c)?

options.text: (r - 1, c), (r + 1, c), (r, c - 1), and (r, c + 1), subject to bounds.
Only the four diagonal cells.
Every unvisited cell in the grid.
Only (r, c) again.

feedback.reason: Candidate generation begins with the exact movement relation.
feedback.details: Bounds, target matching, and visited checks then filter these four potential neighbors.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.all_grid_cells: A path step must satisfy adjacency.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.same_cell: The current cell cannot be reused as the next step.

items.prompt: Why may the same board cell be used in two different successful paths returned by a collect-all solver?

options.text: Visited restrictions apply within one path, not necessarily across independent result paths.
Visited state is ignored after the first solution.
Every cell has an implicit duplicate copy.
All result paths are one shared mutable path.

feedback.reason: Path-local visited state prevents cycles and reuse inside a branch while allowing independent branches to use the same resources.
feedback.details: The result contract determines whether paths are independent and whether global resource consumption exists.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.visited_ignored: Each path must still obey no-reuse internally.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.cell_duplicates: The same physical cell is reused across separate branches.

items.prompt: A solver temporarily replaces board[r][c] with a sentinel to mark it visited. What must happen when the branch returns?

options.text: Restore the original cell value.
Leave the sentinel for all future searches.
Sort the row containing the cell.
Remove the cell from the grid.

feedback.reason: In-place marking is a reversible state representation.
feedback.details: The frame that changes the cell owns the restoration, including failure and any success path that must preserve the input.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.leave_sentinel: That converts path-local visited state into a global block.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.sort_row: Sorting is unrelated and changes the board.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.delete_cell: The grid shape must remain unchanged.

items.prompt: Why should the outer driver try every grid cell as a potential starting position when no start is specified?

options.text: A valid path may begin at any cell matching the first target condition.
Every recursive function must be called once per cell.
Global visited state requires visiting every cell first.
Trying every cell sorts the grid.

feedback.reason: The first choice is the starting cell.
feedback.details: Each start launches an independent path-local search unless the contract specifies a fixed origin.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.recursion_requires_all: This is problem-specific, not a recursion rule.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.visited_global: The outer loop should not globally mark cells.

items.prompt: Which checks can reject a grid-search call before marking the current cell? Select all that apply.

options.text: The coordinates are outside the grid.
The cell does not match the required target symbol or predicate.
The cell is already visited by the current path when reuse is forbidden.
A different neighbor failed in an earlier sibling branch.

feedback.reason: Immediate legality checks occur before mutating visited state.
feedback.details: Sibling outcomes do not create global illegality.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.neighbor_failed: One sibling's failure does not invalidate this cell.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.out_of_bounds: There is no legal cell to use.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.target_mismatch: The branch cannot represent the target prefix.

items.prompt: Which state must be restored between sibling grid branches? Select all that apply.

options.text: The current cell's visited mark.
Any temporary sentinel written into the board.
Any coordinate or character appended to the current path.
The immutable target string itself.

feedback.reason: Every mutable branch-local representation must return to the parent state.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.immutable_target: Reading shared immutable input requires no restoration.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.visited_mark: Sibling paths need the parent visited set.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.board_sentinel: The original cell value must be restored.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.path_entry: The sibling should inherit only the parent path.

items.prompt: Which tests are important for grid-search visited-state correctness? Select all that apply.

options.text: A one-cell grid with matching and non-matching targets.
A valid path that changes direction.
A target that can match only if a cell is illegally reused.
Only one large random board.

feedback.reason: Minimal, directional, and illegal-reuse cases expose distinct state errors.
feedback.details: Large random tests are supplementary rather than sufficient.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.only_large_random: It may not isolate boundary and restoration defects.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.single_cell: It tests completion and minimal bounds.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.requires_turn: It verifies neighbor enumeration beyond a straight line.

items.prompt: Arrange the control flow for one grid-search call.

elements.text: Return success if the target is already fully matched.
Reject bounds, mismatch, or path reuse.
Mark the current cell as used by the path.
Search neighbors, then restore the cell before returning.

feedback.reason: Completion, immediate failure, mutation, and recursive expansion have distinct ordering.
feedback.details: This sequence avoids invalid accesses and preserves path-local state.

items.prompt: Arrange the operations for exploring one legal neighbor.

elements.text: Compute the neighbor coordinates.
Check bounds, target progress, and visited state.
Recurse into the legal neighbor.
Process the result according to find-one or collect-all semantics.

feedback.reason: A neighbor becomes a child branch only after its coordinates and state are legal.
feedback.details: The result contract determines whether success short-circuits or enumeration continues.

items.prompt: Arrange the reversible in-place marking sequence for a cell.

elements.text: Save the original cell value.
Replace it with a visited sentinel.
Explore all legal descendants of the current path.
Write the original value back before returning.

feedback.reason: In-place marking must preserve enough information to reverse the mutation.
feedback.details: Restoration belongs to the frame that introduced the sentinel.

items.prompt: Arrange the outer search over unspecified starting cells.

elements.text: Iterate every grid coordinate.
Skip starts that cannot match the first target condition.
Run a fresh path-local backtracking search from the candidate start.
Return early for find-one or accumulate results for collect-all.

feedback.reason: The start position is the root choice of each independent path search.
feedback.details: Cheap first-symbol filtering avoids launching impossible roots.

items.prompt: Arrange failure unwinding after all neighbors of a marked cell fail.

elements.text: Determine that no neighbor completed the target.
Restore the current cell's visited state.
Remove any current-cell path entry if one was recorded.
Report failure to the parent branch.

feedback.reason: The parent must receive failure together with its original branch state.
feedback.details: Returning before restoration leaks the failed path into sibling exploration.

items.prompt: A grid has R·C possible starting cells, the target length is L, and each step considers at most four neighbors. Select a standard loose worst-case time bound and recursion depth.
constraints: A cell cannot be reused within one path.
The bound may ignore the reduced branching after the first step.

feedback.reason: Each starting cell can launch an exponential path search bounded by target length.
feedback.details: No-reuse often lowers the effective branching factor, but O(RC·4^L) is a common safe upper bound.

items.prompt: A grid search uses an explicit R by C visited matrix and recursion depth at most L, excluding the immutable board and returned results. Select the auxiliary-space bound.
constraints: The visited matrix remains allocated for the search.
Only one depth-first path is active at a time.

feedback.reason: The explicit visited representation and recursion stack both contribute.
feedback.details: Reversible in-place marking can remove the separate RC visited matrix, but still needs O(L) stack depth.
